head.hi: The Acting Secretary of State to Minister Rockhill.

div.p: Sir: I have to acknowledge the receipt of your dispatch No. 353, of the 18th ultimo, transmitting correspondence with the consul-general at Hankow in regard to the regulations for governing the commercial settlement of Changteh Fu, in Honan, and your objections to some of them, and to inform you that your objections commend themselves to the department and are in accord with its telegram of May 8 last, directing that in discussing regulations for international settlements at Antung and Mukden the American agent object to irksome Chinese supervision and arbitrary municipal regulations.

If Americans are to secure proper treatment at Mukden and Antung, the best way to do so is to begin by insisting in every case that regulations which may become a precedent for future ports shall contain no objectionable features.
